infinite_loop_broken.notes: askQuestion stable ([] deps via mutationRef) → useEffect([open, askQuestion]) only fires on open change → loop broken

mutationRef_sync_pattern.notes: Synchronous render-body assignment is canonical React pattern. Superior to useEffect for mutation ref.

hasAskedInitialRef_pattern.notes: useRef correct — flag is not reactive/JSX-consumed. No re-render needed.

rules_of_hooks.notes: All hooks at top level, consistent order, no conditional hooks.

edge_cases.notes: Open/close quickly: safe. In-flight mutation: safe (AnimatePresence keeps component alive). N parallel panels: no cross-contamination.

stale_closures.notes: All captured values are refs (always current). setMessages stable by React contract. Zero stale closure risk.

pointer_events_animation.notes: initial/exit: none, animate: auto. TypeScript 'as const' correct for Framer Motion props.

typescript_build.notes: Build passes. No any types. Proper typing throughout.

root_cause_analysis.original_bug: contextChatMutation (tRPC useMutation) returns new object every render → askQuestion useCallback([contextChatMutation]) recreated every render → useEffect([open, hasAskedInitial, initialPrompt, askQuestion]) re-fires every render → setMessages([]) + setHasAskedInitial(false) → re-render → infinite loop
root_cause_analysis.impact: N AnomalyCards × 1 InlineAiPanel each = N parallel infinite setState loops → React event queue saturated → ALL navigation/click events unresponsive
root_cause_analysis.fix_correctness: mutationRef pattern correctly breaks the cycle at its root: stable askQuestion → effect only fires on open change → no spurious re-renders

positive_findings: Excellent code comments explaining WHY the pattern is used
mutationRef synchronous assignment is superior to useEffect approach
useRef for hasAskedInitialRef is semantically correct — internal flag, not reactive
pointerEvents defensive fix prevents click-through during animations
Effect declaration order ensures initialPromptRef is updated before open effect fires
